citation_short: IDF History Dept., Study of Six-Day War Fatalities
title: Research on the Fatalities of the Six-Day War
year: n.d. (modern historical study based on 1967 records)
date_scope: War-related fatalities from wounds received 5-20 June 1967
strengths: Strongest accessible source for Israeli military fatalities split by front; based on official personnel/casualty records.
limitations: Counts only Israeli military fatalities; not civilians. Egypt-front tabulation may mix Sinai and Gaza, limiting pure-Sinai use.
notes: Key table gives totals of 340 on the Egypt front, 289 on the Jordan front, 149 on the Syria front, and 781 overall.

title: Volume 1 Table I: West Bank Population According to 1967 Census and Jordanian 1961 Census
author_or_org: Israel Central Bureau of Statistics / Levy Economics Institute archival copy
strengths: Direct front-relevant population denominator; explicitly excludes East Jerusalem and reports both pre-war and post-war counts.
limitations: Post-war census reflects wartime displacement after fighting; not a daily wartime exposure series.
notes: Totals are 598,637 in the 1967 census and 729,804 in the 1961 Jordanian census.

title: Volume 6 Table A: East Jerusalem
author_or_org: Israel Central Bureau of Statistics / Levy Economics Institute archival copy
strengths: Direct East Jerusalem denominator with both pre-war and post-war counts.
limitations: Some values are flagged for high sampling error; still a post-war census and not a daily wartime presence series.
notes: East Jerusalem total is 75,000 in the 1961 Jordanian figure and 65,857 in the 1967 count.

citation_short: Jerusalem Institute, Jerusalem on the Map
title: Jerusalem on the Map: Basic Facts and Trends, 1967-1996
strengths: Clean city-level population series; provides 1967 total and Jewish/Arab split.
limitations: Reported for unified Jerusalem after 1967; Jewish total is used here as a proxy for West/Jewish Jerusalem exposure and therefore still needs a modeling note.
notes: 1967 Jerusalem total 266.3 thousand; Jews 197.7 thousand; Arabs and others 68.6 thousand.

title: The Jordanian Front
strengths: Clear front-specific narrative and deployment summary.
limitations: Not a primary document; should be used critically and cross-checked.
notes: States Jordan had ten of eleven brigades in the West Bank totaling about 45,000 men; Israel initially had only three infantry brigades and one armored brigade on this front.

title: 1967 - 40 years of occupation
geographic_scope: All fronts, especially Jordanian and Syrian front timing
strengths: Compact date-coded front timeline with troop count on the Jordanian front.
limitations: Timeline journalism rather than a technical order-of-battle source.
notes: Reports that on 7 June, 40,000 troops and 200 tanks were deployed against the Jordanian army.

title: Six-Day War (overview and West Bank fighting snippets)
pages_or_sections: infobox and West Bank fighting snippets
geographic_scope: War-wide overview plus Jordanian front civilian impacts
strengths: Provides compact, citable summary values when cleaner front-specific sources are absent in the accessible set.
limitations: Tertiary and internally inconsistent in places; used here only as a low-confidence anchor or broad bound.
notes: Useful details include 15 civilians killed in Jerusalem, 1 killed in Netanya, a broader report of 20 Israeli civilian dead and over 1,000 wounded, Jordanian losses of 696-700 killed, and Syrian strength/casualty ranges in the infobox.

title: Facts on File, 14 June 1967 issue on the Arab-Israeli war
pages_or_sections: Jordanian Front section; Sinai-Suez Front section; Syrian Front section
strengths: Near-contemporaneous front chronology, ceasefire timing, and widely cited casualty statements including Gavish's Sinai estimate.
limitations: Digest reporting rather than archival microdata; contains claims from belligerents that must be classified critically.
notes: Useful for 7 June ceasefire timing on the Jordanian front and for Gavish's estimate of 7,000-10,000 Egyptian soldiers killed in the Sinai campaign.

title: The Egyptian Front
pages_or_sections: balance of forces; concluding casualty summary
strengths: Provides clear front-level strength and casualty summary for Sinai.
limitations: Secondary synthesis; mixes Sinai and the broader Egyptian front in places, and reports Nasser's statement for Egyptian losses.
notes: Gives almost 100,000 Egyptian troops in Sinai, 45,000 Israelis initially facing them, 275 Israeli dead, and more than 11,500 Egyptian dead per Nasser.

title: The Syrian Front
pages_or_sections: force balance and concluding casualty summary
strengths: Front-specific narrative distinguishing early shelling from the later Israeli assault; provides troop and casualty figures.
limitations: Secondary synthesis; Israeli military deaths for the Golan operation (115) do not perfectly match the broader IDF Syria-theater total (149).
notes: States over 40,000 Syrian troops in the Golan, Israeli losses during the Golan operation of 115 killed and 306 wounded, and Syrian losses estimated at 2,500 killed.

title: Displacement in the Heights: How the Population of the Golan Heights Vanished in 1967
pages_or_sections: Population in the Syrian Golan - 1967; final figures of general census
strengths: Archival investigation using declassified and ICRC-linked material; gives strong pre-/post-occupation population anchors.
limitations: Documents depopulation and census endpoints, not direct civilian deaths during the assault itself.
notes: Reports roughly 90,000 residents in the Syrian Golan on 1 June 1967 and 6,396 still counted after the August Israeli census.

title: The Fall of Jerusalem, 1967
author_or_org: S. Abdullah Schleifer / Journal of Palestine Studies
pages_or_sections: middle sections on neighborhood fighting and local armed volunteers
strengths: Rich eyewitness detail on East Jerusalem fighting and civilian participation in combat.
limitations: Not a casualty registry; status is ambiguous because it often describes armed civilian volunteers and snipers rather than clearly separated civilians or soldiers.
notes: Crucial for the decision to keep at least 100 armed civilian deaths in East Jerusalem as unknown status rather than folding them into civilian deaths.

title: Demographic Consequences of the Occupation
geographic_scope: West Bank, East Jerusalem, and Gaza
strengths: Useful secondary discussion of pre-war Palestinian population and displacement scale after June 1967.
limitations: Retrospective demographic analysis rather than a front-day wartime count; broader than the 3-day episode used here.
notes: Supports the view that large post-war displacement affected the 1967 census and that pre-/post-war bounds should frame the West Bank denominator.
